premises.content: CF3-PA passivated Pb-Sn perovskite films exhibit effective carrier lifetime of 966 ns, compared to 437 ns (PA), 365 ns (PEA), and 159 ns (control), as measured by time-resolved photoluminescence
premises.label: carrier_lifetime_966_ns

premises.content: Ab initio molecular dynamics at 400 K show all 16 CF3-PA cations adsorb completely on the perovskite surface, compared to 15/16 for PA and 13/16 for PEA, indicating near-complete defect coverage during film formation
premises.label: complete_surface_coverage

premises.content: XRD patterns show that adding trace amounts of CF3-PA does not form 2D perovskite phases even at 20 mol% concentration, maintaining pure 3D perovskite structure beneficial for charge transport through thick absorber layers
premises.label: no_2d_phase_formation

premises.content: CF3-PA passivated single-junction Pb-Sn PSCs with 1.2 um absorber achieve best PCE of 22.2% (stabilized 22.0%), with Voc=0.841 V, Jsc=33.0 mA/cm2, FF=80%, and average PCE of 20.8+/-0.5% across 237 devices
premises.label: pb_sn_psc_22_pct

premises.content: Angle-dependent XPS measurements confirm that CF3-PA passivation successfully suppresses surface Sn2+ oxidation in Pb-Sn perovskite films by passivating undercoordinated Sn atoms and Sn vacancies
premises.label: sn_oxidation_suppressed

premises.content: DFT calculations show CF3-PA has the strongest binding energy with acceptor-type defects (FA vacancy, Sn vacancy, I_Sn, I_Pb antisites) on Pb-Sn perovskite surfaces among all three passivators, and eliminates deep in-gap states from antisite defects
premises.label: strongest_binding_energy

premises.content: CF3-PA not only increases adsorption probability on perovskite surfaces but also suppresses iodide ion desorption at 400 K, reducing formation of both iodine vacancies and iodine interstitial defects
premises.label: suppressed_iodine_vacancies
